events.title: Ringo Starr turns 86
events.blurb: Sir Richard Starkey, better known as Ringo Starr, the legendary drummer for The Beatles, celebrates his 86th birthday.

angles.name: Drumming Legend
angles.copy: Happy 86th birthday to the one and only Ringo Starr! From the Fab Four to his All-Starr Band, his rhythm is unmistakable.

events.title: Brian May turns 79
events.blurb: Queen's iconic guitarist, songwriter, and astrophysicist Brian May celebrates his 79th birthday.

angles.name: Guitar God
angles.copy: Happy 79th birthday to the legendary Brian May! His guitar riffs with Queen are truly out of this world.

events.title: Mick Jagger turns 83
events.blurb: Sir Mick Jagger, the charismatic frontman of The Rolling Stones, celebrates his 83rd birthday.

angles.name: Rock 'n' Roll Icon
angles.copy: Still moving like Jagger at 83! Happy birthday to the incomparable Mick Jagger, a true rock 'n' roll legend.

events.title: MTV's 45th Anniversary
events.blurb: Music Television (MTV) launched on August 1, 1981, forever changing the music landscape with its first video, The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star'.

angles.name: Video Killed The Radio Star
angles.copy: 45 years ago today, MTV launched with 'Video Killed The Radio Star'. The irony isn't lost on us, but what a moment for music!

events.title: Oasis' 'Definitely Maybe' turns 32
events.blurb: Oasis' groundbreaking debut album 'Definitely Maybe' was released on August 29, 1994, becoming a Britpop defining moment.

angles.name: Britpop Classic
angles.copy: Thirty-two years since Oasis dropped 'Definitely Maybe'. Which track still gives you that supersonic feeling?

events.title: Woodstock Festival's 57th Anniversary
events.blurb: The legendary Woodstock Music & Art Fair, a pivotal moment in counterculture history, took place from August 15-18, 1969.

angles.name: Peace & Music
angles.copy: 57 years since half a million gathered for three days of peace and music at Woodstock. A festival that truly shaped a generation.

events.title: 49th Anniversary of Elvis Presley's Death
events.blurb: Today marks 49 years since the world lost the 'King of Rock 'n' Roll', Elvis Presley, who passed away on August 16, 1977.

angles.name: Long Live The King
angles.copy: Remembering the King today, 49 years since Elvis Presley left the building for good. His legacy still shakes, rattles, and rolls.

events.title: 30 Years Since Tupac Shakur Shooting
events.blurb: While the calendar entry is September 1st, Tupac Shakur was tragically shot in a drive-by on September 7, 1996, in Las Vegas, and died six days later on September 13, 1996.

angles.name: Remembering Tupac
angles.copy: Thirty years since the tragic shooting that led to the loss of hip-hop icon Tupac Shakur. His music and message live on.

events.title: 56th Anniversary of Jimi Hendrix's Death
events.blurb: While the calendar entry is September 2nd, the music world mourns the 56th anniversary of the death of guitar legend Jimi Hendrix, who passed away on September 18, 1970.

angles.name: Guitar God Gone Too Soon
angles.copy: 56 years since we lost the incomparable Jimi Hendrix. His innovative guitar work continues to inspire generations.

events.title: Beatles' 'Abbey Road' Turns 57
events.blurb: While the calendar entry is September 4th, The Beatles' iconic album 'Abbey Road' was released in the UK on September 26, 1969.

angles.name: Iconic Album Cover
angles.copy: The Beatles' 'Abbey Road' turns 57. That album cover is still one of the most recognisable images in music history.

events.title: Chrissie Hynde turns 75
events.blurb: Chrissie Hynde, the formidable frontwoman of The Pretenders, celebrates her 75th birthday.

angles.name: Pretenders Power
angles.copy: Happy 75th birthday to the incredible Chrissie Hynde! A true rock icon, still delivering those powerful Pretenders hits.

events.title: Liam Gallagher turns 54
events.blurb: Liam Gallagher, the distinctive voice of Oasis and a Britpop legend, celebrates his 54th birthday.

angles.name: Mad For It
angles.copy: Happy 54th birthday to the one and only Liam Gallagher! Still bringing that rock 'n' roll swagger. As you were.

events.title: John Lennon's 'Imagine' Turns 55
events.blurb: While the calendar entry is October 1st, John Lennon's iconic song 'Imagine' was released as a single in the US on October 11, 1971.

angles.name: Imagine Peace
angles.copy: Fifty-five years since John Lennon asked us to 'Imagine'. A timeless message still resonating today.

events.title: First iPod Turns 25
events.blurb: While the calendar entry is October 3rd, Apple unveiled the first iPod on October 23, 2001, and it was released on November 10, 2001, revolutionising how we carry our music.

angles.name: 1000 Songs In Your Pocket
angles.copy: Remember the first iPod? Twenty-five years since Apple changed the game, putting '1,000 songs in your pocket'. What was on your first one?

events.title: James Bond Day
events.blurb: October 5th is James Bond Day, marking the 64th anniversary of the premiere of the first James Bond film, 'Dr. No', in 1962.

angles.name: Shaken, Not Stirred
angles.copy: Happy James Bond Day! Celebrating 64 years of 007, from iconic gadgets to unforgettable theme tunes. What's your favourite Bond moment?

events.title: John Lennon would have been 86
events.blurb: Remembering John Lennon, who would have celebrated his 86th birthday today. The co-founder of The Beatles was born on October 9, 1940.

angles.name: Remembering A Legend
angles.copy: On what would have been his 86th birthday, we remember the visionary John Lennon. His music and message continue to inspire.
